title: Post Fiat Signal Routing Protocol
description: Canonical pre-flight routing contract for the Post Fiat network. Defines how a producer decides which signals to EMIT, WITHHOLD, or INVERT before sending to downstream consumers. Companion to pf-signal-schema (what a signal looks like) and pf-resolution-protocol (how a signal is scored). This protocol defines the decision layer between signal generation and signal emission. Any producer can adopt this protocol to replace ad-hoc filtering with auditable, evidence-based routing.

routing_action.description: Routing verdict for a candidate signal. EMIT = send as-is (maps to signal schema action=EXECUTE). WITHHOLD = suppress (maps to signal schema action=WITHHOLD). INVERT = flip direction before sending (maps to signal schema action=INVERT). The routing protocol uses EMIT rather than EXECUTE to distinguish the routing decision from the signal-level action field.

gate_result.description: Result of a single pre-flight gate check. Each gate is a binary pass/fail test with the threshold that was applied and the actual value that was measured.

passed.description: Whether the signal passed this gate.

threshold.description: The threshold value or condition that was applied. Type varies by gate: number for numeric thresholds, string for enum checks, object for compound conditions.

actual.description: The actual measured value for this signal. Same type as threshold for comparability.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of why the gate passed or failed.

voi_computation.description: Value-of-Information computation for a single signal. VOI = E[karma|send] - E[karma|withhold]. A signal should be sent iff VOI > 0. The VOI incorporates accuracy, confidence, and optionally a hazard adjustment for regime duration.

e_karma_send.description: Expected karma if signal is sent. E[karma|send] = confidence * (2 * P(correct) - 1) for a given (symbol, duration_bucket) cell. Positive when accuracy > 50%, negative when accuracy < 50%.

e_karma_withhold.description: Expected karma if signal is withheld. Always 0.0 (not sending = no karma change). Included explicitly so the decision framework is transparent.

voi.description: Value of information: e_karma_send - e_karma_withhold. Positive = sending adds expected value. Negative = withholding is optimal.

accuracy_estimate.description: Estimated P(correct) for this (symbol, duration_bucket) cell. Source: historical proof surface or backtest.

sample_size.description: Number of historical signals in this cell. Low sample sizes (< 30) reduce reliability of the VOI estimate.

hazard_adjustment.description: Optional Weibull hazard adjustment. Higher hazard = regime more likely to end = signal less reliable.

hazard_rate.description: Weibull hazard h(t) = (k/lambda) * (t/lambda)^(k-1) at the current regime duration.

survival_probability.description: Weibull survival S(t) = exp(-(t/lambda)^k). Probability the regime persists.

hazard_adjusted_voi.description: VOI discounted by survival probability: voi * S(t).

weak_symbol_diagnosis.description: Diagnosis of symbol-level weakness. A weak symbol has accuracy consistently below 50%, making it a candidate for INVERT or WITHHOLD. Composite score from 5 components: accuracy_deficit, reliability_gap, karma_drag, brier_excess, directional_bias.

weakness_score.description: Composite weakness score (0 = strong, 1 = maximally weak). Weighted sum of 5 components.

severity.description: Human-readable severity. NONE < 0.20, MILD < 0.40, MODERATE < 0.60, SEVERE >= 0.60.

ci_lower.description: Wilson CI lower bound on accuracy.

ci_upper.description: Wilson CI upper bound on accuracy.

ci_excludes_50.description: Whether the entire 90% CI is below 50%. Strong evidence for inversion.

inversion_justified.description: Whether INVERT is statistically justified (CI excludes 50% AND n >= 100).

inversion_p_value.description: Two-proportion z-test p-value for inversion improvement.

components.description: 5-component breakdown. Keys: accuracy_deficit, reliability_gap, karma_drag, brier_excess, directional_bias. Each has score (0-1) and weight.

routing_decision.description: Complete routing decision for a single candidate signal. Includes the verdict, all gate results, optional VOI computation, and audit trail.

signal_id.description: ID of the candidate signal being routed.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol of the candidate signal.

confidence.description: Original confidence of the candidate signal.

direction.description: Original direction of the candidate signal.

routed_direction.description: Direction after routing. Same as original for EMIT, flipped for INVERT, absent for WITHHOLD.

gates_failed.description: Gates that the signal failed. If any gate fails, the signal is withheld (unless overridden by INVERT logic).

voi.description: VOI computation details (present when VOI gate is configured).

weak_symbol.description: Weak symbol diagnosis (present when the symbol has a configured weakness policy).

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of the routing decision. Single sentence summarizing why this signal was emitted, withheld, or inverted.
examples: INVERT: SOL weakness SEVERE (0.70), CI excludes 50%, p=0.0001

routing_report.title: Routing Decision Report
routing_report.description: Complete pre-flight routing report for a batch of candidate signals. Primary output of preflight_filter.py. Contains per-signal routing decisions, summary statistics, and the routing configuration used.

producer_id.description: Producer whose signals are being routed.

signal_schema_version.description: Version of pf-signal-schema the input signals conform to.

config_version.description: Version of the routing configuration used.

filter_rate.description: Fraction of signals withheld: total_withhold / total_input.

invert_rate.description: Fraction of signals inverted: total_invert / total_input.

per_gate_pass_rates.description: Pass rate per gate across all signals. Keys are gate IDs, values are pass rates (0-1).

per_symbol_summary.description: Per-symbol routing breakdown. Keys are symbol tickers.

decisions.description: Per-signal routing decisions in input order.

config_snapshot.description: Snapshot of the routing configuration used for this report. Enables audit: anyone can reproduce the routing decisions given the same config + input signals.
